entity.title: Realm
entity.description: A namespace in which Entity Unique Identifiers are unique and Rule Identifier Format is consistent. The organizational boundary for rule authoring.

rules.body: IS a namespace in which Entity Unique Identifiers are unique.
HAS a Rule Identifier Format configuration.
MUST contain a `_schema/` directory with schema files used to validate its entities.
MUST contain a `realm.json` manifest at its root.
MAY declare dependencies on other Realms, importing their rules as read-only references.
MUST NOT define Entity Unique Identifiers that collide with any Realm it depends on.
An Entity MUST reside in the directory tree of exactly one Realm.
MUST have an identifier that follows reverse domain notation (e.g., `com.example.realm`) to ensure global uniqueness across organizations.
Cross-realm entity references MUST use the qualified form `<realm-id>:<EUID>`. Within the same Realm, the short form (EUID only) is sufficient.

entity.title: Realm Manifest
entity.description: The realm.json file that declares a Realm's identity, version, and dependencies.

rules.body: IS a JSON file named `realm.json` at the root of a Realm directory.
MUST declare the Realm's identifier, title, and version.
MAY declare dependencies on other Realms with pinned version references.
MAY override the Rule Identifier Format defaults for the Realm.
